entry.title: Re: How to add signature document generated through 'open...
entry.content:
Hi @johnchristhuraj45 ,

Please find below insights on how to handle this.

Step-by-Step Approach:
1. OpenText and SuccessFactors Integration Setup
Ensure that OpenText Extended ECM for SAP SuccessFactors is implemented.

This standard integration enables documents generated by OpenText to be stored in the Extended ECM repository and linked back to SuccessFactors.

2. Document Generation Process
During the Recruiting or Onboarding process, relevant data (Job Info, Personal Info, etc.) flows from SuccessFactors to OpenText.

OpenText generates the personalized document (e.g., contract).

The generated document is automatically stored in the ECM repository, with metadata linked to the corresponding employee/onboardee record.

3. Presenting Documents to Onboardee
There are two main options to present these documents to the onboardee:

Option A: Using Onboarding 2.0 Document Management
In Onboarding 2.0, you can configure the Document Flow or Document Management Step.

During this step, you can present the document(s) to the onboardee for review, acknowledgment, or signature.

To achieve this:

The OpenText-generated document must be stored with proper linkage to the onboardee's record.

SuccessFactors can be configured to pull the document from OpenText repository, or upload it back to SuccessFactors (via integration or manually) to make it available in the Document Management step.

Example:

Candidate completes New Hire tasks.

System generates Contract via OpenText.

Document stored in OpenText, linked to Onboardee.

In the Onboarding 2.0 Document Management step, the document is presented for acknowledgment or e-signature.

Option B: Direct Link to OpenText via Tile or Portlet
Alternatively, create a Custom Tile or Link within the Onboarding portal that redirects the onboardee to their documents stored in OpenText.

This requires:

The onboardee having appropriate permissions to access their specific document.

OpenText providing secure, individual-specific URLs for each document.

Note: This approach depends on OpenText configuration and security policies.

4. Technical Prerequisites & Considerations
✔ OpenText Extended ECM for SAP SuccessFactors license and integration must be active.
✔ Ensure metadata mapping between SuccessFactors and OpenText is properly configured.
✔ For seamless experience:

Use the Onboarding Document Flow step for document presentation.

Automate the document movement from OpenText back to SuccessFactors (if needed) via APIs or integration.

5. Common Real-Time Scenarios
Scenario Solution
Offer Letter Generation OpenText generates → Available in Onboarding Document Step.
Employment Contract OpenText generates → Linked via Document Management or custom tile.
Acknowledgment Required Use Document Management step for acknowledgment/signature.

Recommendations
✔ If OpenText and SuccessFactors are tightly integrated, use the Document Management step within Onboarding 2.0 — this is standard, user-friendly, and secure.
✔ If integration is partial, explore storing documents in OpenText and providing secured links to onboardees.
✔ Engage OpenText and SAP consultants for any custom development or API automation.

To present OpenText-generated documents to onboardees in SuccessFactors:

✔ Leverage the Onboarding 2.0 Document Management step to present documents during the onboarding process.
✔ Ensure proper integration between OpenText and SuccessFactors, with documents linked to the onboardee.
✔ Explore secured URL or tile options for direct access if document movement to SuccessFactors is not feasible.

Hope this clear and let me know if any queries.

Thanks,

kr,

karthick.

entry.title: Facing Issue in GOS Attachment for MM03
entry.content:
This Code works fine without any error but there is no attachment against a given material in mm03 ,

Please help me to identify the issue:

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& Report ZZ_TEST_GOC
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*&
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
REPORT ZZ_TEST_GOC.

DATA: lv_matnr      TYPE matnr,
      lv_xstring    TYPE xstring,
      lv_objlen     TYPE i,
      lt_solix      TYPE solix_tab,
      lt_obj_cont   TYPE soli_tab,
      lt_obj_head   TYPE soli_tab,
      ls_hd_dat     TYPE sood1,
      ls_fol_id     TYPE soodk,
      ls_doc_id     TYPE soodk,
      ls_object     TYPE borident,
      ls_note       TYPE borident,
      ls_object_a     TYPE borident,
      ls_object_b     TYPE borident,
      lv_log_handle   TYPE balloghndl,
      lt_return       TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapiret2,
      lv_msg_text     TYPE string.


DATA : gv_name           TYPE pcfile-path,
       gv_ext            TYPE char4,
       gv_path           TYPE string,
       gv_filename1       TYPE string,
       gv_down           TYPE string,
       gv_bin_file       TYPE xstring,
       gv_key            TYPE char8,
       gv_vbeln          TYPE vbeln,
       gv_msgty          TYPE msgty,
       gv_message        TYPE string,
       gv_len            TYPE i,
       gt_tab            TYPE tsfixml,
       gv_str            TYPE string,
       gv_filename       TYPE string,
       gv_filename_dummy TYPE string.

DATA: gs_exc           TYPE REF TO cx_root,
      gt_return        TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapiret2,
      gt_per_info      TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF smum_xmltb,
      gs_per_info      TYPE smum_xmltb,
      gt_bindata       TYPE solix_tab,
      gv_form          TYPE xstring,
      gv_output_length TYPE i.

SELECTION-SCREEN BEGIN OF BLOCK b1 WITH FRAME.
  PARAMETERS : p_upload TYPE rlgrap-filename OBLIGATORY.
SELECTION-SCREEN END OF BLOCK b1.

  CALL METHOD cl_gui_frontend_services=>file_save_dialog
    EXPORTING
      default_extension    = 'PDF'
      file_filter          = cl_gui_frontend_services=>filetype_all
    CHANGING
      filename             = gv_filename_dummy
      path                 = gv_filename_dummy
      fullpath             = gv_filename
    EXCEPTIONS
      cntl_error           = 1
      error_no_gui         = 2
      not_supported_by_gui = 3
      OTHERS               = 4.


AT SELECTION-SCREEN ON VALUE-REQUEST FOR p_upload .

  CALL FUNCTION 'F4_FILENAME'
    IMPORTING
      file_name = p_upload.

AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT.

  IF p_upload IS NOT INITIAL.

    CALL FUNCTION 'SO_SPLIT_FILE_AND_PATH'
      EXPORTING
        full_name     = p_upload
      IMPORTING
        stripped_name = gv_name
        file_path     = gv_path
      EXCEPTIONS
        x_error       = 1
        OTHERS        = 2.

    CALL FUNCTION 'PC_SPLIT_COMPLETE_FILENAME'
      EXPORTING
        complete_filename = gv_name
      IMPORTING
        extension         = gv_ext
      EXCEPTIONS
        invalid_drive     = 1
        invalid_extension = 2
        invalid_name      = 3
        invalid_path      = 4
        OTHERS            = 5.
    IF sy-subrc <> 0.
* Implement suitable error handling here
    ENDIF.

    TRANSLATE gv_ext TO UPPER CASE.

    IF gv_ext NE 'PDF'.
      MESSAGE 'Selected file is not PDF.' TYPE 'W' DISPLAY LIKE 'E'.
    ENDIF.

  ENDIF.

START-OF-SELECTION.

  IF p_upload IS NOT INITIAL.

    gv_filename1 = p_upload.

    CALL METHOD cl_gui_frontend_services=>gui_upload
      EXPORTING
        filename   = gv_filename1
        filetype   = 'BIN'
      IMPORTING
        filelength = gv_output_length
      CHANGING
        data_tab   = gt_bindata[].

    IF sy-subrc = 0.

      CALL FUNCTION 'SCMS_BINARY_TO_XSTRING'
        EXPORTING
          input_length = gv_output_length
        IMPORTING
          buffer       = gv_bin_file
        TABLES
          binary_tab   = gt_bindata
        EXCEPTIONS
          failed       = 1
          OTHERS       = 2.
    endif.
endif.
lv_matnr = 'DS-13D80260'.
lv_xstring = gv_bin_file.
" Convert MATNR to 18-char format
CALL FUNCTION 'CONVERSION_EXIT_ALPHA_INPUT'
  EXPORTING input = lv_matnr
  IMPORTING output = lv_matnr.

" Get SAPOffice folder
CALL FUNCTION 'SO_FOLDER_ROOT_ID_GET'
  EXPORTING region = 'B'
  IMPORTING folder_id = ls_fol_id.

" Convert XSTRING to SOLIX
CALL FUNCTION 'SCMS_XSTRING_TO_BINARY'
  EXPORTING buffer = lv_xstring
  IMPORTING output_length = lv_objlen
  TABLES binary_tab = lt_solix.

" Convert SOLIX to SOLITAB
CALL FUNCTION 'SO_SOLIXTAB_TO_SOLITAB'
  EXPORTING ip_solixtab = lt_solix
  IMPORTING ep_solitab  = lt_obj_cont.

" Prepare document metadata
ls_hd_dat-objdes   = 'Uploaded File'.
ls_hd_dat-objlen   = lv_objlen.
ls_hd_dat-objpri   = '5'.
ls_hd_dat-file_ext = 'PNG'. " or PDF, etc.

APPEND VALUE #( line = |&SO_FILENAME=Uploaded_File.PNG| ) TO lt_obj_head.
APPEND VALUE #( line = |&SO_FORMAT=BIN| ) TO lt_obj_head.

" Insert document
CALL FUNCTION 'SO_OBJECT_INSERT'
  EXPORTING
    folder_id        = ls_fol_id
    object_hd_change = ls_hd_dat
    object_type      = 'EXT'
    owner            = sy-uname
  IMPORTING
    object_id        = ls_doc_id
  TABLES
    objcont          = lt_obj_cont
    objhead          = lt_obj_head.

" Link document to material (GOS)
ls_object-objtype = 'BUS1001006'.
ls_object-objkey  = lv_matnr.

ls_note-objtype   = 'MESSAGE'.
ls_note-objkey    = ls_doc_id.


CALL FUNCTION 'BINARY_RELATION_CREATE_COMMIT'
  EXPORTING
    obj_rolea    = ls_object
    obj_roleb    = ls_note
    relationtype = 'ATTA'
  EXCEPTIONS
    OTHERS       = 1.


IF sy-subrc <> 0.
  " Step 3: Create BAL log
  CALL FUNCTION 'BAL_LOG_CREATE'
    EXPORTING
      object     = 'ZMAT_ATTACH'
      subobject  = 'ATTACHMENT'
    IMPORTING
      log_handle = lv_log_handle.

  " Step 4: Add error message
  CALL FUNCTION 'BAL_LOG_MSG_ADD'
    EXPORTING
      log_handle = lv_log_handle
      msgid      = 'ZMSG'         " Your custom message class
      msgno      = '001'          " Message number
      msgty      = 'E'
      msgv1      = 'Attachment failed for material'
      msgv2      = lv_matnr.

" Step 5: Save log
  CALL FUNCTION 'BAL_DB_SAVE'
    EXPORTING
      log_handle = lv_log_handle.
ELSE.
  COMMIT WORK.
  MESSAGE 'Document successfully linked to material' TYPE 'S'.
ENDIF.

entry.title: Re: account assignment error after OKB9 MAINTENANACE.
entry.content:
Hello @NIRAJ2 

In SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, you cannot use OKB9 — default account assignments are maintained through SSCUI 150000 - Default Account Assignment for Cost Elements.

Your error means the system needs a controlling object (like a Cost Center) for that GL account but couldn’t find one.

  • Go to Manage Your Solution
  • Use SSCUI 150000
  • Maintain a default assignment for your company code and GL account

This replaces OKB9 in Public Cloud. If SSCUI 150000 isn’t available, request access via your admin.

KBA 2628966 – Error: Account & requires an assignment to a CO object

Hope it helps

With kind regards

Chuma

entry.title: SAP MM Enterprise Structure: The Unilever Global
entry.content:
📌What is enterprise structure in SAP MM?

The enterprise structure in SAP MM represents the organizational hierarchy in your business. It's the foundation of how materials are procured, stored, and managed.

Each component of the structure reflects a real-world business unit, such as a company, plant, or storage location.

🎯Enterprise Structure vs Organizational Structure—Difference

🧱 Key Elements of SAP MM Enterprise Structure

Let’s break it down:

Component Description
ClientTopmost level in SAP. Represents the entire corporate group.
Company CodeIndependent legal accounting entity. Required for all financial transactions.
PlantRepresents a location where materials are produced or stored.
Storage LocationSubdivision of a plant, where inventory is stored physically.
Purchasing OrganizationHandles procurement activities for one or more plants.
Purchasing GroupGroup of buyers or a buyer responsible for procurement.
 

⚙️SAP Configuration: Step-by-Step Setup

Here’s how you configure enterprise structure in SPRO (SAP Customizing Implementation Guide):

 

🌐Real-Time Example

Imagine a multinational company like Unilever:

 

 

📊SAP Tables for Enterprise Structure

 

💡Pro Tips for Consultants

  • Always plan your enterprise structure before starting configuration.

  • Use naming conventions (e.g., IN01 for the India plant) to keep things organized.

  • Understand the business process before mapping the SAP structure.

🔄Types of Procurement Structures in SAP MM

SAP allows three types of procurement strategies:

  1. Plant-Specific Procurement
    Each plant has its own purchasing organization.

  2. Cross-Plant Procurement
    One purchasing organization serves multiple plants in the same company.

  3. Cross-Company Procurement
    One central purchasing organization serves plants across different company codes.

🏁Final Words

Setting up the SAP MM Enterprise Structure correctly is the foundation for all downstream processes like procurement, inventory management, and invoice verification. Whether you're a fresher, a consultant, or an enterprise architect—mastering this is non-negotiable.

📚Interview Questions (Must-Know!)

1. What is the organizational structure in material management (MM module)?
2. What are the organizational levels of the enterprise structure in R/3?
3. Define "client." What is its importance in SAP?
Define "company." How is it different from a client? What are the Data in the MM Module that are
Maintained at the company code level?
5. How do we create a company code in SAP?
6. How many charts of accounts can be assigned to a company?
7. How many company codes can be assigned to one chart of accounts?
8. How many company codes can be assigned to a company?
9. What is a plant in the MM module?
10. What are the prerequisites for creating a plant?
11. Can storage locations be created automatically?
12. Can Two Plants Have a Common Storage Location?
13. How is a storage location assigned to a plant?
14. What is a purchasing organization in SAP?
15. What are the different ways of organizing purchasing organizations?
16. What is the reference purchasing organization in SAP?
17. What is the Purchasing Value Key?
18. How Can We Configure the Purchasing Value Key?
19. How is a purchase group defined in SAP?
20. How can you set user defaults for views and organizational levels?
22. What is the menu path to configure storage location?
23. How is storage location defined in SAP?
24. What is the menu path to assign a company code to a plant?
25. Which organizational unit is entered when creating a purchasing view of any material?
26. What are valuation areas?
27. What is a chart of accounts in SAP?
28. What is a factory calendar?
29. What is the relation between a controlling area and a company code?
30, What are the options in SAP when it comes to fiscal years?
31. What is a year-dependent fiscal year variant?
32. How many currencies can be configured for a company code?
33. If there are two company codes with two different chart of accounts, how you can consolidate their
activities?
34. How does posting happen in MM during special periods?

entry.title: Re: TCS Configuration for Scrap Sales in India (SAP Publi...
entry.content:
Hello @Vishal41 

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (India), TCS on Scrap Sales follows standard India localization settings.

1% TCS without PAN → This works out of the box if PAN is missing in the Customer Master (Identification tab).
0.1% TCS after ₹50 lakh threshold → The system doesn’t track turnover thresholds automatically in Public Cloud. You need to handle this with:

  • Custom Logic using BAdI /LOCF/IN_TAX_ADDITIONAL_DATA for TCS based on turnover
  • Or manual reporting and adjusting TCS condition records as needed

Check the SSCUIs to below,

  • 102378 – Define Tax Codes
  • 101505 – Maintain Tax Condition Records
  • 100383 – Assign Tax Codes in Sales Processes

TCS Condition Type: Usually pre-delivered as JTCS, ensure it’s assigned to your Scrap Sales pricing procedure.

Note

  • TCS rate based on PAN works standard 
  • Threshold-based TCS (₹50 lakh) needs BAdI or manual control
  •  SAP does not yet provide automated threshold checks for TCS in the Public Cloud

Check below SAP Help reference
India Localisation – S/4HANA Public Cloud

With kind regards

Chuma

entry.title: Re: How to Assign Business Partner to Communication Syste...
entry.content:
Hello @sanjaynaiksap 

It’s not possible to assign a Business Partner to a Communication System using CBO or In-App ABAP Extensibility in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

This assignment is strictly controlled via the Communication System app & Communication Arrangement app. There is no supported option to manage this through custom coding, CBOs, or ABAP in the Public Cloud.

Possibilities:

  • You can manage BPs using standard APIs (e.g., API_BUSINESS_PARTNER)
  • You can set up Communication Systems and Arrangements manually or with supported tools, but not through custom logic

SAP Reference,

SAP Help – Communication System Setup in S/4HANA Public Cloud

For full automation, SAP recommends using the Integration Suite or pre-delivered content.

With kind regards

Chuma

entry.title: Re: Debugging a Python SAP BTP Service Locally in VSCode
entry.content:
Yes, we can do the same in Business Application Studio (BAS); I just double-checked.


There are a few small differences:
1. You need to explicitly install the 'Python debugger' extension. Before the installation, you will be asked to confirm that you understand the potential risks.

2. You must use a different port instead of 3000 (e.g., 4000), otherwise you'll encounter an 'address already in use' issue. This port can either be defined in the .env file as the 'PORT' variable or hard-coded in the code.

3. To test your service in the browser, you need to use a specific URL, such as:
https://port4000-workspaces-ws-*....*.us10.trial.applicationstudio.cloud.sap/
You don’t have to memorize this address — just click the button in the bottom-right corner once your script is running.
(This applies to testing any application or service locally in BAS.)

Please refer to this blog for information on how to set up a Python environment in Business Application Studio.

entry.title: Huge spike in amounts in F.19
entry.content:
Hello everyone!

Our client is facing a problem with F.19. Each month, they run it to see the differences between GR and IR. They don't use it to post any documents. They compare the GNB and BNG amounts and for the most part the amounts and the difference has been reasonable by their standards. This means that there have been for the most part invoice receipts for most good receipts. However, as of like 4 months, there has been a huge spike in the amounts all of a sudden. We tried controlling the PO documents to see if we can see anything weird but nothing. We tried changing the sorting key in the GR/IR account to 014( PO) but the amounts are still getting bigger. Another strange thing we have is that on the F.19 report we get, there's a line at the top like this: operation : ** GNB (the other lines have* GNB or * BNG) , Supplier: empty field, PO number: XX, and the amount in this line is huge, like 9 million. Our client interpreted this as the report being too big that it couldn't display all lines, so SAP added the amounts on a bunch of lines together. This doesn't seem correct. I'm not that well familiar with F.19 and would really appreciate if someone could enlighten me on the specifics of this Tcode. The client are really in a tough spot as they can't post these amounts that became huge all of a sudden to the GR/IR account, especially since they can't explain them.
Has anyone here faced a similar situation? what was the root cause of it and how did you fix it?

PS:

  • We asked the end users if they usually use tcode F.13 but apparently they don't.
  • They are using ECC

entry.title: Problem in archivelink
entry.content:
Hello all!

Our client has reported the following problem. They use an invoice cockpit to process invoices and post accounting documents. The posted accounting documents usually contain as attachment the scanned invoice. They used to be able to go to Tcode FB03, view attachments and once they click on the document, it opens up directly and they can view it as they please. Also, the name of the document used to contain a distinction between the invoices and the other types of attachments. However and for a while now, this is no longer the case. The document titles are no longer descriptive of the document type and they have to export the document locally to be able to view it and see what it is. This is obviously as lot of work and is frustrating. Here's what we've done/found so far:

  • In table TOA01, the content repository being used has been changed a while ago
  • Following this discovery, we tried to transfer this topic to the BASIS team but they sent it back saying that the problem doesn't come from their end as the content repositories are well maintained. So, we are on our own here.
  • It was suspected that the problem comes from the fact that the user doesn't have OpenText on their computer. The IT team fixed this but the problem still exists.
  • We have noticed in TOA01 also that for a while now, all documents have document type Z**PFR, this used to be Z**PDF. This probably means nothing as the description for both document types is cockpit document PDF. InTOA01, documents under both document types have PDF as a reserve (I don't know if this means anything)
  • We did a test: we exported a document locally, then imported the same document as an attachment, and we can view this document on SAP by double clicking on it. It is imported as an adobe document. 
  • We have looked into ArchiveLink configuration: OAC0, OAC1, OAC2, OAC3 and even OAAD. But as functional consultants, we couldn't see anything wrong in them.
  • All old attachments are inaccessible because according to the error message they were archived. I think the repository in which they're stored is no longer accessible.

Has anyone faced a similar problem? If so, how did you fix it? What are the potential root causes of such a problem? Where can we look? 

Thank you in advance for your help, I really appreciate it.

entry.title: Re: How to use MATERIAL_SAVE_CONFIGURATION
entry.content:
Since, the link provided here as the solution doesn't work anymore and also considering the fact this thread is shown as one of the most relevant results whenever we search for FM 'MATERIAL_SAVE_CONFIGURATION' for updating the Material Configurations details, am updating the correct solution here:

1.) SAP Note 969755 : This contains a sample SAP shipped source code for mass maintenance of material variant configurations.

2.) SAP Note 736873 : This Note would help you understand the relevance of various parameters and how it need to be used for above BAPI FM. Just go through the description of this note, the main part is the below details:

        TABLES 
          "To maintain cross-plant configurations
          e1cucfg         = lt_e1cucfg
          e1cuins         = lt_e1cuins
          e1cuval         = lt_e1cuval
          e1cucom         = lt_e1cucom
          "To maintain plant-specific configurations
          e1cucfg_w       = lt_e1cucfg
          e1cuins_w       = lt_e1cuins
          e1cuval_w       = lt_e1cuval
          e1cucom_w       = lt_e1cucom
*          "To maintain plant-specific configurations for planning variants
*         e1cucfg_v       = lt_e1cucfg
*         e1cuins_v       = lt_e1cuins
*         e1cuval_v       = lt_e1cuval
*         e1cucom_v       = lt_e1cucom

Hope this helps someone struggling with the FM.

Thanks,

Sijin

entry.title: Cannot Update Content Package Due to ID Mismatch in SAP Build Work Zone
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I'm currently working with SAP Build Work Zone and encountered an issue while trying to update an existing content package.

I’m getting the following error message:

"Trying to upload a content package with a different ID than the ID of the existing content package."

Here is what I’ve done:

entry.title: Re: Diagnostics Relevant
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

This is Haterm and I can help with your query. 
The 'Diagnostics-Relevant' is a checkbox in Software tab of registered system information in transaction code LMDB. When the indicator is marked, the selected Product Instance will try to search its RCA templates. The RCA templates will be used for data collection for RCA functions and relevant monitoring functions. So the 'Diagnostics-Relevant' is required for configuring managed systems into Solution Manager landscape.
Usually, the 'Diagnostics-Relevant' will be checked in step 1 & 2 of Managed Systems Configuration for managed systems. If the 'Diagnostics-Relevant' is not marked, a warning message will be raised and displayed in step 1 & 2 of Managed Systems Configuration.

Best regards,
Haterm

entry.title: Re: UPLOAD DATA FROM EXCEL TO ANY TABLE Z IN SAP
entry.content:
Date / time validation

CLASS zcl_check_valid_date_time DEFINITION
  PUBLIC
  FINAL
  CREATE PUBLIC .

  PUBLIC SECTION.
    INTERFACES if_oo_adt_classrun.

  PROTECTED SECTION.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
ENDCLASS.



CLASS zcl_check_valid_date_time IMPLEMENTATION.

  METHOD if_oo_adt_classrun~main.

    DATA(lv_date) = CONV d( '20251232' ).   "Incorrect date
    "DATA(lv_date) = conv d( '20250229' ).   "Incorrect date
    "DATA(lv_date) = CONV d( '20251231' ).   "Correct date
    "DATA(lv_date) = conv d( '20250228' ).   "Incorrect date

    "DATA(lv_time) = '245960'.              "Incorrect time
    DATA(lv_time) = CONV t( '235959' ).      "Correct Time

    CONVERT DATE lv_date TIME lv_time
      INTO TIME STAMP DATA(lv_timestamp)
      TIME ZONE 'UTC'.

    IF sy-subrc <> 0.
      out->write( 'Date or time not valid.' ).
      RETURN.
    ENDIF.

    out->write( 'Date and time are valid.' ).
    out->write( |Timestamp { lv_timestamp }| ).

  ENDMETHOD.

ENDCLASS.

Currency

Use CDS View I_Currency (which uses DB table TCURC)

entry.title: Re: how to display technical field name on Fiori
entry.content:
You could determine the database table by the next steps.

  1. Starting the browser debugger
  2. Change the value Sold to party.
  3. Click on another field. SAP now send the new value to the backend.
  4. See the debugger

  6. Open the service binding: C_SALESORDERMANAGE_SRV
  7. Drill down from the Service Binding to...
    1. Service definition: C_SALESORDERMANAGE_SD
    2. CDS view: C_SalesOrderManage
  8. Set cursor on field: SalesOrder.SoldToParty
  9. Press F3 (Navigate to...) till you find: 
    vbak.kunnr as SoldToParty,
    1. CDS views: C_SALESORDERMANAGE -> R_SALESORDERTP -> I_SALESORDER -> I_SALESDOCUMENT -> I_SALESDOCUMENTBASIC

entry.content:
Hello Community,

I am currently working with SAP Customer Checkout and have a question regarding the stock overview functionality.

At present, when we view the stock overview for a specific article, the system displays the total available stock per warehouse. However, we are using batch-managed items (e.g., clothing articles with color or size variations managed via batches), and we would like to see the stock overview broken down by batch instead of just the total quantity in the warehouse.

Is there a way in SAP Customer Checkout to view the stock overview batch-wise, so that we can monitor inventory at the batch level (e.g., stock per color or size for a specific article)? If this is not supported by default, is there any configuration that might enable this?

Looking forward to your suggestions or experiences.

Best regards,

Moazzam Baig

entry.title: Re: "Method not Allowed" when posting Fixed Asset API
entry.content:
Hello @JastinC1 

The Fixed Asset API in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition doesn’t let you create an asset with a simple POST to /Fixed_Asset. Instead, SAP requires you to trigger a specific bound action called

/Fixed_Asset('<asset_key>')/SAP__self.CreateMasterFixedAsset

What you should do

  • Postman works — because you call the right action path
  • ABAP Service Consumption Model may not support calling bound actions directly (a common Public Cloud limitation)
  • If you need this from ABAP
  • Check if you can create a request for the action explicitly
  • If not supported, open a ticket with SAP to clarify

For now, you might want to handle is via

  • SAP API Hub or Postman for testing
  • Integration tools (like SAP BTP or middleware) for production calls

Helpful References:

  • SAP API Hub – Fixed Asset API
  • SAP Help – Service Consumption Model Basics
  • SAP Note 3096196 – OData V4 API Restrictions

The API needs the action path for asset creation. ABAP may not support it directly yet, so either confirm with SAP or run it via integration tools.

With regards

Chuma

entry.title: Re: Customer UUID
entry.content:
I got it working finally:

Therefore we need 2 steps:

1. Extract UUID with 'Query Accounts' web service(extracts all customer account UUIDs):

<soapenv:Envelope xmlns:soapenv="http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/soap/envelope/" xmlns:glob="http://sap.com/xi/SAPGlobal20/Global">
   <soapenv:Header/>
   <soapenv:Body>
     <glob:CustomerByElementsQuery_sync>
      <CustomerSelectionByIdentification>
        <SelectionByInternalID>
          <InclusionExclusionCode>I</InclusionExclusionCode>
          <IntervalBoundaryTypeCode>1</IntervalBoundaryTypeCode>
          <LowerBoundaryInternalID>1000000</LowerBoundaryInternalID>
          <UpperBoundaryInternalID>99999999</UpperBoundaryInternalID>
        </SelectionByInternalID>
      </CustomerSelectionByIdentification>
    </glob:CustomerByElementsQuery_sync>

   </soapenv:Body>
</soapenv:Envelope>

2. Update Communication method to 'Do Not Send' for particular business document (Sales Quote in my case. Updating just for one Customer):

<soapenv:Envelope xmlns:soapenv="http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/soap/envelope/" xmlns:glob="http://sap.com/xi/SAPGlobal20/Global" xmlns:glob1="http://sap.com/xi/AP/Globalization">
   <soapenv:Header/>
   <soapenv:Body>
         <glob:CustomerBundleMaintainRequest_sync_V1>
         <BasicMessageHeader/>
         <Customer actionCode="02">
            <UUID>059511d6-d7e7-1edf-9db7-c820327f841c</UUID>

        <CommunicationArrangementMultiChannel actionCode="02" communicationChannelListCompleteTransmissionIndicator='true'>
          <CompoundServiceInterfaceCode>46</CompoundServiceInterfaceCode>
          <EnabledIndicator>true</EnabledIndicator>
           <CommunicationChannel actionCode="02">
            <CommunicationMediumTypeCode>NOT</CommunicationMediumTypeCode>
            <OutputRequestFormTemplateCode>C0002</OutputRequestFormTemplateCode>
          </CommunicationChannel>
        </CommunicationArrangementMultiChannel>

        </Customer>
        </glob:CustomerBundleMaintainRequest_sync_V1>
      
  </soapenv:Body>
  </soapenv:Envelope>

entry.title: Re: DRC
entry.content:
Hello @Sunny10 

Yes, there are some key differences between DRC (Document and Reporting Compliance) in Public Cloud and Private Cloud:

In S/4HANA Public Cloud, DRC is delivered as a standardised, pre-configured solution with limited flexibility; you can use the available reports and integration options, but deep custom development or country-specific extensions are restricted.

In Private Cloud, you have more control over customising, extensions, and technical setup, allowing for broader adaptations to meet specific legal or business requirements.

The core compliance functions are similar across both, but Public Cloud follows stricter standardisation to ensure upgrade stability.

Reference:
SAP Help – Document and Reporting Compliance  SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition using Germany as a case study

Hope that helps clarify!

Thank you and kind regards

entry.title: Re: Custom tokens in Onboarding
entry.content:
Hi @piotr_retecki ,

In SuccessFactors Onboarding 2.0, handling custom tokens for onboarding emails where multiple Job Relationships are maintained is a known limitation area. Here's a clear explanation and potential solutions based on standard system behavior and possible workarounds:

System Limitation
Tokens in Onboarding emails pull data from standard fields available in Process Objects or Direct Mapping.

For Job Relationships, if multiple entries exist (e.g., multiple Managers, HR, Matrix Managers), the system always picks the first record by default.

There is no native way to dynamically select a specific job relationship for a token based on type (e.g., Direct Manager, HR Manager, Matrix Manager) unless that relationship is mapped directly to a dedicated field.

Recommended Solution in Onboarding 2.0
Mapping Job Relationship Fields to Dedicated Process Object Fields
During Recruit-to-Hire Data Mapping or Manage Data Collection, map specific Job Relationships (e.g., Direct Manager, Matrix Manager, HR Manager) to dedicated custom fields in the Onboarding Process Object.

These dedicated fields (e.g., cust_directManagerName, cust_HRManagerName) can then be exposed as tokens in Onboarding emails.

Example Setup
Job Relationship Type Target Field in Process Object Token in Email
Direct Manager cust_directManagerName [[cust_directManagerName]]
HR Manager cust_HRManagerName [[cust_HRManagerName]]

Steps to Achieve This
Go to Recruit-to-Hire Mapping or Data Collection Configuration.
Create custom fields in the Onboarding Process Object to store the specific Job Relationship names.
Use Business Rules during data collection or mapping to fetch the correct name based on the Job Relationship Type.
Reference those custom fields as tokens in your onboarding email templates.

Why Direct Token Mapping to Job Relationships Fails
The Job Relationships object is a composite/multivalued association.

Tokens don't natively handle filtering or conditional selection within such associations.

Hence, system only picks the first record, regardless of type.

Alternative (Less Recommended) Options
Manual Entry During Onboarding
If feasible, collect the required names manually during the onboarding data collection step.

Custom MDF Object Approach
Maintain a custom object that stores the specific relationship names in a controlled way and map those to the Onboarding Process.

Key Considerations
This method requires configuration adjustments in Onboarding 2.0 Process Objects and Email Services.

Coordination with your Implementation Partner or Admin with access to Data Models is essential.

Ensure you test end-to-end to confirm correct values appear in the generated emails.

Conclusion
You cannot directly control which Job Relationship is pulled by tokens due to system limitations.
Best practice is to map specific job relationships to dedicated fields, then use those fields as tokens in email templates.
This method is widely used in SuccessFactors Onboarding 2.0 to overcome this token limitation.

Hope this helps!!

Thanks,

kr,

karthick.

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud Access Controls cannot get transported: Obj...
entry.content:
Hello @DanielSchneider 

Good question, this is expected behaviour in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

Access Policies (Object Type: DRAS) cannot be transported between tenants, such as from Development to Quality or Production. This is by design, as certain security-related artefacts like access policies must be created and maintained separately in each tenant to ensure system stability and compliance.

In your case, the error message:

"Object DRAS ZBUSINESSPARTNEROFUSER is not permitted. Object type DRAS cannot be transported"

is consistent with this restriction.

Next Steps:
You’ll need to manually recreate the Access Policy in the target tenant
Raising an SAP Incident is recommended to confirm this limitation in your tenant and check for any roadmap updates

SAP Help reference below,
SAP Help – Extensibility Restrictions (S/4HANA Cloud)

Hope this helps to clarify!

With regards

Chuma

entry.title: Retrofit setup - project landscape - Domain link
entry.content:
Hi All,

I have configured the retrofit and while saving configuration following message is showing.

For security reasons I have given the source SID as ABC for BAU and XYZ as project landscape in this forum.

BAU Source system: ABC ~ ABAP/200 ; 

Project landscape target system : XYZ / 200

We have only built DEV (XYZ) in project landscape and configured QAS as a virtual system (yet to build).

So while saving the Retrofit configuration, below warning message occurred.

================================================================

Diagnosis

Systems ABC~ABAP/200 and XYZ~ABAP/200 are not in the same domain and there is no domain link between them.

 

System Response

You may experience problems when using the retrofit functions.

 

Procedure

Linking two domains with a domain link involves two steps:

 

1. Request a link between two domains.

2. Confirm the link between the two domains.

Requesting a Link Between Two Domains

To request a link between two transport domains, proceed as follows:

 

1. Log on to one of the two domain controller systems.

2. Call transaction STMS.

3. Choose Overview -> Systems.

The system overview appears.

4. Choose SAP System -> Create -> Domain link.

The dialog box Request for Linking 2 Domains appears.

5. Enter the system name, host name, and system number of the domain controller you want to link to and confirm your entries.

Your SAP System performs the following actions automatically:

Generates the required RFC destinations

Sends the address data of the controller to the controller in the other domain.

You can now see that you have requested the domain link to the other domain in the system overview.

For security reasons, you now need to confirm the link between the two domains in the other controller.

Confirming a Link Between Two Domains

To confirm a link between two transport domains, proceed as follows:

1. Log on to the domain controller in the other domain.

2. Call transaction STMS.

3. Choose Overview -> Systems.

The system overview appears.

4. Position the cursor on the domain controller where you requested the domain link and choose SAP System -> Approve.

5. Confirm the prompt and distribute the configuration.

The two domain controllers now exchange all necessary information about the systems in their domains. This information is distributed to all systems in the domain whose controller you are currently logged on to. A transport profile is generated, which contains all systems in both domains.

The information about the systems in the other domain is not automatically distributed to the systems in the domain where you requested the domain link. This means that you must distribute the new configuration to these systems.

===========================================================================

Solution Manager is our domain controller. We have added / configured XYZ (project landscape DEV system) into Domain Controller (solution Manager).

My question is ,

Though Solman and XYZ are in same domain controller, do we have to create a domain link between solman and XYZ system (project landscape system)?

entry.title: Empowering SAP Service Cloud V2 with OpenText: Seamless Integration for Document Management
entry.content:
In today's digital-first customer service landscape, managing documents efficiently is crucial for ensuring speed, compliance, and seamless service delivery. SAP Service Cloud V2 now supports powerful integration with OpenText, enabling agents to securely manage, store, and retrieve customer documents within a single interface. This blog outlines the key steps to configure OpenText with SAP Service Cloud V2 and highlights how the integration streamlines customer interactions.

📌 Why OpenText + SAP Service Cloud V2?

The integration enables organizations to:

  • Centralize document storage and access directly within SAP.
  • Ensure compliance with data governance and retention policies.
  • Provide agents with a 360° view of customer interactions—including documents, cases, and correspondence.
  • Automate document capture, archiving, and retrieval processe

🔧 Prerequisites: What You Need Before Integration

Before starting the configuration, ensure you have the following:

OpenText Extended Account Access

  • Administrator credentials to access the OpenText .
  • API and endpoint URLs.

SAP Service Cloud V2 Tenant Details

  • V2 instance with admin access.
  • Role-based authorization to manage integration settings.

OpenText Endpoint (Base URL) for Mashup

  • Example: https://contentserver.opentext.example.com
    Purpose: This acts as the destination for all document management operations.

Authentication Credentials (for Testing)

  • Username: <OpenTextUser>
  • Password: <OpenTextPassword>
    These credentials will be used to authenticate API calls from SAP to OpenText during testing and validation.

SAP Domain Whitelisting (Trusted Origin)

  • Your SAP Service Cloud domain must be added as a trusted origin within the OpenText configuration and vice versa. This ensures secure cross-origin communication between the two platforms.

Folder & Document Structure Setup (Recommended)

  • Predefine folder structures and metadata rules in OpenText to simulate realistic document storage scenarios during integration testing. This helps ensure documents are correctly categorized, archived, and retrieved within SAP Service Cloud V2.

Step 1: Configure the Open Text mashup 

  1. Log in to SAP Service Cloud.
  2. Click on the user icon (top-right corner) and select Settings.
  3. Navigate to All Settings → Extensibility → Mashup Authoring.
  4. Add the Mashup

Step 2: Adjust Content Security Policies

To ensure the CloudFront domain is trusted by SAP Service Cloud:

  1. Go to Settings → Content Security Policy Settings.
  2. Add the CloudFront domain (e.g., opentext.com) under all relevant sections to whitelist it.

Step 3: Add Mashup in case the End-to-End Integration

  • Embed the OpenText mashup within the Case screen for easy access.
  • Seamlessly navigate to the OpenText Tab document in SAP Service Cloud.
  • Choose the appropriate communication channel for document storage or retrieval.

  • select the channel

  • Upon clicking the Submit button, the document is generated and added to the 'General → Attachments' section

The reference document can be shared with the customer via the case email channel, allowing the process to continue seamlessly in alignment with the company's defined workflow

📌 Conclusion

Integrating OpenText with SAP Service Cloud V2 provides a unified digital workspace for agents—combining customer data, case insights, and document management in one platform. This not only boosts operational efficiency but also strengthens compliance, transparency, and customer satisfaction.

If you're looking to enhance your SAP V2 experience, OpenText integration is a strategic step toward intelligent service management.

entry.title: 🚀 What’s New in Cloud Control Center PL21 – Smarter, Safer, and More in Control
entry.content:
Cloud Control Center PL 21

📅Released: June 23, 2025

📍Target audience: SAP Business One Partners, Cloud Operators, SAP Business One cloud customers and SAP Business One fan base in general.😎

Last week we launched SAP Business One Cloud 1.1 Patch Level 21 (PL21) — and if you’re using Cloud Control Center (CCC) to manage your SAP Business One Cloud landscape, this update is worth your attention.

PL21 is packed with enhancements focused on security, visibility, automation, and flexibility.

👉 In this post, I've rounded up 12 of the most impactful and attractive highlights — the ones i believe will make the biggest difference in your day-to-day operations.

Here’s a quick walk-through of what’s new 👇

 

🔐Certificate Expiration – Now Fully Visible

No more surprises!

The CCC home page now displays certificate expiration dates for your key components (License Servers, Service Layers, Job Services, etc.).

✨Bonus:

An automatic scan is triggered when CCC starts. You’ll get a warning if a certificate is expired or due to expire within the next 30 days — and yes, this can be toggled off in global settings if needed.

 

🔍See SLD Agent Versions at a Glance

You asked, we delivered — SLD Agent version info is now shown in the component details. Easier troubleshooting, less back-and-forth.

🧹 CCC Cleans Up After Itself

Every time you open CCC, it automatically removes logical machine logs older than 6 months.

You can also now remove unused logical machines more easily:

  • If the SLD Agent status is unreachable
  • If no SLD Agent is installed, just restart the SLD service to clean it up.

🔐Dynamic Key Encryption – Now Available for Tenants

Dynamic Key encryption can now be activated for tenants directly in CCC.
Strengthen your security posture without hassle.

 

⚠️UAP/CCC Version Mismatch Warning

If there’s a version mismatch between User Access Portal (UAP) and CCC, you’ll now see a clear warning.

A small heads-up that could save you from bigger issues.

 

⬆️Enforce Tenant Upgrades via Staging Units

A new global setting now lets you restrict tenant upgrades to staging service units only.

This adds a layer of control that’s especially useful in multi-tenant environments.

 

📊Analytics Services Initialization During Upgrade

When duplicating or upgrading tenants on SAP HANA service units, a new checkbox lets you automatically initialize the tenant in Analytics Services.

✅Less manual setup

✅Faster analytics availability

 

🔐Certificate Verification Built-In

Whether you’re installing CCC, UAP, or Tenant Configurator, new steps in the wizard ensure you only proceed with valid certificates in place. It’s a simple but vital enhancement.

 

💾Automatic B1AS Backup During Upgrade

During upgrades of CCC, a new wizard step now backs up the Authentication Service database (B1AS) — just in case. 🙌

 

📈High Availability Setup – Now Streamlined

We’ve made it easier to set up HA for your B1 Cloud components:

  • Simpler nginx reverse proxy installation
  • Updated SLD HA config files
  • No need to choose Admin/Worker mode during install — all nodes are now equal

Less complexity. More flexibility.

 

👥Operator Access with Boundaries

Need to involve someone in SAP Business One implementation without authorization to change CCC settings?

You can now assign the “Read-Only and Open PSU Connection” role — perfect for implementation experts who need access to the product but shouldn’t change cloud settings.

🆕One More Thing…

SAP Business One Cloud 1.1 PL21 now officially supports Microsoft SQL Server 2022.

That’s one more way we’re keeping your platform options open.

💬Your Feedback Matters

We invite you to try out the new features in Cloud Control Center PL21 and tell us what you think!

What’s working well? What would you love to see next?

👇Drop your thoughts in the comments — we’re always listening.

entry.title: invalid lob locator id (piecewise lob reading) Error after upgrading to CAP 9.0.3 and Node.js 20
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am encountering a critical error in my CAP Node.js application after upgrading my dependencies. I would appreciate any help or insights you can provide.

Problem Description:

My Node.js service, which uses @sap/cds, fails with the following error when trying to read an Excel file from a HANA database BLOB column:

error while parsing protocol: invalid lob locator id (piecewise lob reading)

The error occurs specifically in the readExcelSheets function, which reads a Readable stream and converts it into a Buffer. This code worked perfectly before the upgrade.

My Environment & Dependencies:

I recently updated my project to use Node.js 20 and the latest major version of @sap/cds. Here are my relevant dependencies from package.json:

"dependencies": {
  "@cap-js/hana": "^2.1.1",
  "@sap/cds": "^9.0.3",
  "xlsx": "^0.18.5"
},
"devDependencies": {
  "@sap/cds-dk": "^9.0.5",
  "@types/node": "^22.13.1"
}

My SAP HANA Database Version:

My SAP HANA database is running on the following version, as shown in the screenshot from my landscape: 2025.14.5.

Relevant Code Snippet:

The error originates from the following code block in my readExcelSheets function:

 
const readExcelSheets = async (content: Readable | Buffer): Promise<Record<string, any[]>> => {
  let buffer: Buffer;
  if (content instanceof Readable) {
    const chunks: Buffer[] = [];
    for await (const chunk of content) { // <-- The error occurs here
      chunks.push(Buffer.isBuffer(chunk) ? chunk : Buffer.from(chunk));
    }
    buffer = Buffer.concat(chunks);
  } else {
    buffer = Buffer.isBuffer(content) ? content : Buffer.from(content);
  }

  const workbook = XLSX.read(buffer, { type: "buffer" });
  const result: Record<string, any[]> = {};

  // ... rest of the function
  return result;
}

The content variable is fetched from the database using a standard CDS SELECT query:

 
const pendings = await db.run(
  SELECT.from("ExcelUploads")
    .columns("ID", "content", "createdAt")
    .where({ progress: "Pending" })
);

Steps I have taken to troubleshoot:

  1. I initially thought the issue was related to the @sap/cds upgrade.

  2. Based on my research, I suspected a HANA client driver incompatibility with Node.js 20.

  3. I ran npm install @sap/hana-client to update the HANA client driver.

  4. After installing the latest @sap/hana-client (which is likely pulled by @cap-js/hana), I received a new, more specific error:

    [cds] - ❗️Uncaught Error: Invalid Object

    The stack trace points directly to the @sap/hana-client module:

    at Socket.<anonymous> (C:\...\node_modules\@sap\hana-client\lib\index.js:53:13)
at Socket.emit (node:events:519:28)
...
at Readable.push (node:internal/streams/readable:390:5)
at Pipe.onStreamRead (node:internal/stream_base_commons:191:23)
at Pipe.callbackTrampoline (node:internal/async_hooks:130:17) {
  code: -20001,
  sqlState: 'HY000',
  query: false
}
  • Has anyone else experienced this invalid lob locator id or Invalid Object error with @sap/cds 9.x and Node.js 20?

  • Given my HANA version 2025.14.5, is there a specific, compatible version of @sap/hana-client that I should be using with Node.js 20?

  • Is there a CDS configuration setting to alter how BLOB data is read (e.g., to read the entire object at once instead of in pieces)?

Thank you for your time and help!

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud Access Controls cannot get transported: Obj...
entry.content:
Hi Chuma,

thanks for your reply and the provided information!

I found out that the error message was related to another object which was part of the same transport request/task.
The object was an aspect (object type DRAS) which I created first and deleted it right after (since I didn't know how to work with that).

Then I created an access policy (object type DCLS) containing a definition of an aspect inside of it (the one that I posted as screenshot).

As soon as I removed the DRAS object from my transport task, I was able to release it.

So everything is good and there is no issue with the transport request.

Daniel

entry.title: Re: C4C V2 <> S4 Public Prospect to Account confirmation...
entry.content:
Hello @AdityaPatharkar 

This is a known issue when integrating SAP Sales Cloud V2 with S/4HANA Public Cloud via SAP Cloud Integration.

The problem happens because S/4HANA Public Cloud sends a SOAP/XML confirmation, while the standard iFlow expects JSON, leading to the parsing error you’re seeing.

Recommendation 
 Check if you’re using the latest version of the pre-delivered iFlow , recent updates may have addressed payload handling
If the issue persists, raising an SAP Incident is the correct approach, as this likely requires SAP to align the payload formats

You can review SAP Note 3283132 (login required) it addresses payload format mismatches in similar

Thank you and kind regards

Chuma

entry.title: Experience the new dictionary user interface in SAP Signavio Process Collaboration Hub
entry.content:
Overview 

With the SAP Signavio April 2025 Sapphire release, we have published the new dictionary user interface in SAP Signavio Process Collaboration Hub. It improves the usability and user experience for dictionary users within the Collaboration Hub. 

Benefits 

  • In addition to the existing grid view on the dictionary landing page, a unified table view of categories and subcategories is now available displaying the dictionary categories and subcategories in a single table 
  • A detailed and intuitive page for dictionary entries, helping ensure that the relevant information is grouped logically and easily accessible 

Details 

  • The new landing page enables users to choose between grid and table views displaying key meta information (number of subcategories, entries etc.).  

  • Clicking on a dictionary category displays the existing subcategories in grid/table views. The entries present in the category are shown in the table below. Users can collapse the component that displays subcategories to view a greater number of entries. Clicking on a subcategory displays the entries present in that subcategory in a table.  

 

  • Clicking on a dictionary entry displays the details in two tabs: 
    • ‘Attributes’ tab displays all the attributes according to visibility settings

  • ‘Used In’ tab displays the diagrams and dictionary entries that use this dictionary entry. Users can filter conveniently for specific type(s) of link

On all the screens, users can mark or unmark dictionary categories, subcategories and entries as favorites and use the breadcrumbs to navigate easily and navigate to dictionary in SAP Signavio Process Manager to edit or update dictionary entries as needed. 

To help you get familiar with the new dictionary user interface in SAP Signavio Process Collaboration Hub, you can refer to the help documentation  available here.

entry.title: Re: Issue in MIGO Accounting Entry
entry.content:
Hello @sapcomyogik 

Thank you for sharing the details regarding the incorrect accounting entries generated during MIGO postings in your S/4HANA Public Cloud system.

After reviewing your screenshots and based on standard Public Cloud behaviour, this issue is likely related to the incomplete or incorrect setup of Automatic Account Determination, which is configured exclusively through Central Business Configuration (CBC) in the S4HANA Public Cloud environment.

Please note,
In S/4HANA Public Cloud, classic transactions like OBYC are not available.
All account postings related to stock movements (e.g., MIGO) depend on CBC configuration, including transaction keys like BSX, GBB, WRX, etc.
The material master data, especially Valuation Class and Price Control, must also align with CBC settings to ensure correct accounting entries.

Recommended

  • Review your CBC setup for the affected Movement Type and Valuation Class
  • Confirm that the correct G/L accounts are maintained for all relevant transaction keys
  • Validate Material Master data, particularly Valuation Class and Price Control
  • If needed, perform a test posting after adjusting the CBC configuration

SAP Helpful resource
SAP Help – Automatic Account Determination in S/4HANA Cloud

Hope it helps

With regards

Chuma
